timeline.title: started writing things down
timeline.body_md: 5 documents on day one. kept solving the same problems twice. started capturing every fix, every decision, every lesson. just markdown files at first. no structure, no automation — just the habit of not throwing work away.

timeline.title: 168 docs in a week
timeline.body_md: 65 new documents in a single day. the knowledge base went from a habit to a system — vault restructure, obsidian integration, structured types for sessions, fixes, decisions, lessons. 428 KB events that day. it stopped being notes and started being infrastructure.

timeline.title: state your assumptions first
timeline.body_md: 47 sessions in march had the same shape — jumping to code before thinking through the approach. added a rule: state your files, state your approach, state your assumptions, then write. april: 3. the rule stayed.

timeline.title: one place for everything
timeline.body_md: rules were scattered — CLAUDE.md, memory files, random docs. decided KB is the single source of truth. CLAUDE.md became a 15-line bootloader that just points to KB. everything else lives in one searchable place. 578 documents by now.
